gloss.en: a hot-tasting root used in cooking

words.example: She added some ginger to the soup.

gloss.en: a tall African animal with a very long neck

words.example: The giraffe ate leaves from the tall tree.

gloss.en: pleased; happy

words.example: ‘I passed the test!’ ‘I’m so glad.’

gloss.en: a smooth, continuous movement

words.example: The bird made a slow glide over the lake.

gloss.en: a small, quick flash of light

words.example: I saw a glint of gold in the sand.

gloss.en: covering or affecting the whole world

words.example: This year the global economy will grow by about 4 per cent.

gloss.en: a clothing item that covers the hand and fingers

words.example: She wore one glove on her cold hand.

gloss.en: a person's turn in a game or activity

words.example: Whose go is it?

gloss.en: the player who guards the goal in football

words.example: The goalkeeper caught the ball easily.

gloss.en: a female god

words.example: They prayed to the goddess of the sea.

gloss.en: a person who plays golf

words.example: The golfer hit the ball into the hole.

gloss.en: things that are produced to be sold

words.example: The shop sells many kinds of goods.

gloss.en: a large, powerful African ape

words.example: The gorilla sat quietly in the zoo.

gloss.en: talk about other people's private lives

words.example: They love to share gossip about their friends.

gloss.en: a long, formal dress

words.example: She wore a beautiful gown to the party.

gloss.en: moving in a smooth, attractive way

words.example: The dancer was very graceful.

gloss.en: a person who has a university degree

words.example: She is a graduate of a famous university.

gloss.en: the act of completing a course of study

words.example: His family came to his graduation.

gloss.en: a diagram showing how numbers relate

words.example: The graph shows how sales went up.

gloss.en: feeling or showing thanks

words.example: Thank you for doing this. I really am so grateful.

gloss.en: the feeling of being thankful

words.example: She showed her gratitude with a small gift.

gloss.en: a hot sauce made from meat juices

words.example: He poured gravy over the meat.

gloss.en: something you say or do to welcome someone

words.example: She gave me a friendly greeting.

gloss.en: a device for cooking food over heat

words.example: He cooked the fish on the grill.

gloss.en: a man on his wedding day

words.example: The groom waited for the bride.

gloss.en: a set of people or things put together

words.example: The teacher made a new grouping of students.

gloss.en: the process of growing in size or amount

words.example: Lack of water will stunt the plant's growth.

gloss.en: a person who protects a place or people

words.example: The guard stood outside the gate.

gloss.en: an answer given without being certain

words.example: Have a guess at the right answer.

gloss.en: a person who shows others the way

words.example: Our tour guide showed us around the old town.

gloss.en: feeling ashamed for doing something wrong

words.example: Matt and Chrissy both looked equally guilty.

gloss.en: a person who plays the guitar

words.example: The guitarist played a lovely song.

gloss.en: a room with equipment for physical exercise

words.example: The school has recently built a new gym.

gloss.en: physical exercises and body movements as a sport

words.example: She is very good at gymnastics.

gloss.en: a person who cuts and styles hair

words.example: The hairdresser cut my hair short.

gloss.en: a machine that dries hair with warm air

words.example: She used a hairdryer after her shower.

gloss.en: to the extent of half

words.example: The glass was half full.

gloss.en: a tool used for hitting nails

words.example: He used a hammer to fix the chair.

gloss.en: the body part at the end of the arm

words.example: Ian placed a hand on her shoulder.

gloss.en: small enough to be held in the hand

words.example: She used a hand-held camera.

gloss.en: a team sport played with the hands and a ball

words.example: They played handball in the gym.

gloss.en: a small cloth for wiping the nose or face

words.example: He wiped his face with a handkerchief.

gloss.en: the act of shaking hands in greeting

words.example: They greeted each other with a handshake.

gloss.en: the way a person writes by hand

words.example: Her handwriting is neat and clear.

gloss.en: fixed so as to hang down from above

words.example: There was a hanging plant by the window.

gloss.en: the state of feeling or showing pleasure

words.example: to find true happiness

gloss.en: a safe place where ships stay

words.example: The boats stayed safe in the harbor.

gloss.en: putting a lot of effort into work

words.example: She is a hard-working student.

gloss.en: almost not; almost none

words.example: There's hardly any tea left.

gloss.en: a difficult situation, especially without money

words.example: The family lived through years of hardship.

gloss.en: a set of straps to hold or control something

words.example: The climber wore a safety harness.

gloss.en: done too quickly, without enough thought

words.example: He made a hasty decision and later regretted it.

gloss.en: a small opening or door in a wall or floor

words.example: They passed the food through the hatch.

gloss.en: a very strong feeling of dislike

words.example: She gave him a look of real hate.

gloss.en: a safe place; a place of rest

words.example: The park is a quiet haven in the city.

gloss.en: the body part on top of the neck

words.example: She nodded her head in agreement.

gloss.en: the title of a news article in large letters

words.example: The headline was about the big storm.

gloss.en: helping to make someone or something well again

words.example: The warm water had a healing effect.

gloss.en: having a particular kind of heart or feeling

words.example: She is a kind-hearted person.

gloss.en: friendly and full of energy; large and filling

words.example: They gave us a hearty welcome.

gloss.en: a machine that makes a room warm

words.example: She turned on the heater because it was cold.

gloss.en: a system that supplies heat to a building

words.example: We turned the heating on in winter.

gloss.en: very pleasant; of the sky or heaven

words.example: The cake tasted heavenly.

gloss.en: to a great degree; in large amounts

words.example: It was raining heavily.

gloss.en: how tall a person or thing is

words.example: What is the height of this building?

gloss.en: unable to take care of oneself or act

words.example: The baby was small and helpless.

gloss.en: a large group of animals of one kind

words.example: A herd of cows crossed the road.

gloss.en: very; to a high degree

words.example: It is highly unlikely that she'll be late.

gloss.en: important in history
gloss.zh: 有历史意义的；历史性的

words.example: the restoration of historic buildings

gloss.en: connected with the past

words.example: You must place these events in their historical context.

gloss.en: an act of striking somebody or something

words.example: Give it a good hit.

gloss.en: a virus that can lead to AIDS

words.example: Doctors test the blood for hiv.

gloss.en: always telling the truth; not cheating

words.example: He is an honest man.

gloss.en: in a truthful way; really

words.example: Please tell me honestly what you think.

gloss.en: a holiday taken by a newly married couple

words.example: They went to the beach for their honeymoon.

gloss.en: a short jump on one foot

words.example: The bird gave a little hop.

gloss.en: without hope; very bad at something

words.example: The situation seemed hopeless.

gloss.en: very bad or unpleasant

words.example: What horrible weather!

gloss.en: to make someone feel shocked or scared

words.example: The news will horrify her.

gloss.en: a feeling of great shock or fear

words.example: The thought of being left alone filled her with horror.

gloss.en: a cheap place to stay, often for travellers

words.example: We slept in a cheap hostel last night.

gloss.en: the work of cleaning and caring for a home

words.example: They share the housework every day.

gloss.en: extremely large in size or amount

words.example: The sums of money involved are potentially huge.

gloss.en: having warm, wet air

words.example: The weather was hot and humid.

gloss.en: the quality of being funny or amusing

words.example: She has a great sense of humor.

gloss.en: a violent storm with very strong winds

words.example: A powerful hurricane hit the Florida coast.

gloss.en: in a quick and rushed way

words.example: She hurriedly left the room.

gloss.en: the need to do something quickly

words.example: Take your time—there's no hurry.

gloss.en: a small, simple building or shelter

words.example: They lived in a small wooden hut.

gloss.en: who or what somebody or something is

words.example: The police are trying to discover the identity of the killer.

gloss.en: a phrase with a special, non-literal meaning

words.example: "Break a leg" is a common idiom.

gloss.en: a person or thing that is greatly admired

words.example: The singer is a teenage idol.

gloss.en: to pay no attention to something

words.example: He ignored all the ‘No Smoking’ signs and lit up a cigarette.

gloss.en: not allowed by the law

words.example: It is illegal to drive without a licence.

gloss.en: in a way that breaks the law

words.example: The car was parked illegally.

gloss.en: the state of being physically or mentally ill

words.example: The virus can cause illness in humans.

gloss.en: to light up or shine light on something

words.example: Bright lights illuminate the street at night.

gloss.en: existing only in the mind

words.example: The equator is an imaginary line around the middle of the earth.

gloss.en: to copy the way someone acts or speaks

words.example: Children often imitate their parents.

gloss.en: happening or done without delay

words.example: The medicine had an immediate effect.

gloss.en: without delay

words.example: She answered almost immediately.

gloss.en: to come to live in a new country

words.example: They immigrate to find a better life.

gloss.en: the strong effect something has on something

words.example: The new law had a big impact on jobs.

gloss.en: to make something worse or weaker

words.example: Loud noise can impair your hearing.

gloss.en: the quality of being important

words.example: She stressed the importance of careful preparation.

gloss.en: an idea or feeling you get about something

words.example: My first impression of him was favourable.

gloss.en: making you admire because it is very good
gloss.zh: 令人钦佩的；给人深刻印象的

words.example: an impressive performance

gloss.en: not correct or suitable; not honest

words.example: It was improper to ask such a question.

gloss.en: the act or process of getting better

words.example: The economy has shown significant improvement over the past 9 months.

gloss.en: having something as part of a group

words.example: I've got three days' holiday including New Year's Day.

gloss.en: causing trouble or difficulty

words.example: He called at an inconvenient time.

gloss.en: a rise in amount, number or value

words.example: There was a big increase in prices.

gloss.en: very hard to believe; amazing

words.example: an incredible story

gloss.en: extremely; in a way hard to believe

words.example: It was all incredibly difficult.

gloss.en: used to emphasize a statement

words.example: Thank you very much indeed!

gloss.en: having its own government; not relying on others

words.example: an independent state/nation/country

gloss.en: to show that something is true or exists

words.example: Initial tests indicate the presence of oxygen.

gloss.en: not happening as the main cause or result

words.example: the indirect effects of the war

gloss.en: in an indirect way; not directly

words.example: The change affected us indirectly.

gloss.en: considered separately, not as a group

words.example: We interviewed each individual member of the community.
